homeLandingHeroTitle: Efficient medical appointment assignment with AI and human-centered design
homeLandingHeroSubtitle: Asclepio helps reduce missed appointments, optimize medical schedules, and improve the experience for patients and clinical staff.
homeLandingNoShowDescription: We make confirmation and schedule organization easier so care feels smooth for patients and medical staff.
homeLandingSocialProofTitle: Built for healthcare providers that need smooth, bottleneck-free operations
homeLandingServicesDescription: We combine automation, analytics, and multichannel communication to keep scheduling organized and make better use of everyone's time.
homeLandingServicePrimaryDescription: Keeps schedules clear with simple confirmations and quick reassignment when needed.
homeLandingServicePediatricsDescription: Patients request and confirm appointments guided by AI through voice recognition, without phone calls or complex forms.
homeLandingServiceCardiologyDescription: Balance doctor, room, and queue availability to keep operations stable.
homeLandingIntegrationsTitle: Integrations for clinical and operational coordination
homeLandingDoctorsSubtitle: Coordinated roles ensure each appointment has follow-up, confirmation, and timely response.
homeLandingCtaTitle: Turn appointment scheduling into a predictable and efficient operation
homeLandingTestimonialQuote: Scheduling used to feel overwhelming. With Asclepio we can confirm appointments easily, adjust times quickly, and keep care organized for everyone.
homeLandingPlatformTitle: Everything you need to operate efficiently
homeLandingPlatformSlide1Description: Patients schedule, confirm, and cancel appointments from the platform. Medical staff manage availability and reassignments in real time.
homeLandingPlatformSlide2Description: Receptionists create normal, priority, or urgent turns. Nurses call and attend them from their real-time panel.
homeLandingPlatformSlide3Description: CNN chest X-ray classifier. Identifies 6 clinical conditions and generates GRAD-CAM heat maps showing diagnostically relevant regions.
homeLandingPlatformSlide3Feature1: 6 classes: COVID-19, Emphysema, Pneumonia and more
homeLandingPlatformSlide4Description: Doctors record diagnoses, treatments, and notes per patient. The full history is accessible to the authorized care team.
homeLandingPlatformSlide5Title: A waiting room that entertains while it cares
homeLandingPlatformSlide5Description: While patients wait, they play a real-time multiplayer agar.io-style game. When their turn is called, an immediate on-screen notification appears.
homeLandingPlatformSlide5Feature3: Immediate notification when turn is called
homeLandingServiceCard1Description: Patients book with available doctors. Confirmations, cancellations, and rescheduling in one click. Doctors manage their availability slots and offices.
homeLandingServiceCard2Description: Receptionists create Normal, Priority, or Urgent turns. Nurses call and attend from their panel. Patients see their status in real time.
homeLandingServiceCard3Description: CNN classifies chest X-rays into 6 conditions: COVID-19, Emphysema, Normal, Bacterial and Viral Pneumonia, and Tuberculosis. Generates GRAD-CAM heat maps.
homeLandingServiceCard4Description: Doctors log diagnoses, treatments, and notes per patient. History is centralized and accessible to the authorized hospital team.
homeLandingServiceCard5Description: While patients wait, they play a real-time multiplayer agar.io-style game. When called, an immediate on-screen notification appears.
homeLandingServiceCard6Description: Support for multiple hospitals with 5 coordinated roles: Admin, Doctor, Nurse, Receptionist, and Patient. Operational metrics with Prometheus and Grafana.
homeLandingServicesBuiltDescription: A complete hospital platform: 5 coordinated roles, integrated AI, an interactive waiting room, and real-time resource orchestration.

dashboardPatientAiGuidanceSubtitle: General information only. This is not a diagnosis.
dashboardPatientAiGuidanceItem1: If symptoms persist or worsen, consult a healthcare professional for evaluation.
dashboardPatientAiGuidanceItem2: Seek urgent care if you experience severe shortness of breath, chest pain, or confusion.
dashboardPatientAiGuidanceItem3: Keep a record of symptom duration, fever, or recent exposures to support clinical assessment.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseCovidSummary: Respiratory infection caused by SARS-CoV-2 that can affect the lungs and oxygenation.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseCovidSigns: Common reports include fever, cough, fatigue, and shortness of breath.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseEmphysemaSummar: Chronic condition that damages air sacs (alveoli), often linked to smoking or long-term exposure.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseEmphysemaSigns: Typical signs include ongoing shortness of breath, wheezing, and reduced exercise tolerance.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseNormalSummary: No significant abnormalities are visible on the X-ray image.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseNormalSigns: Symptoms can still exist; a normal image does not replace clinical evaluation.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseBacterialSummar: Infection of lung tissue usually associated with localized inflammation.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseBacterialSigns: May present with fever, productive cough, and chest discomfort.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseViralSummary: Lung infection caused by viruses that can overlap with other respiratory illnesses.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseViralSigns: Often includes cough, fever, body aches, and fatigue.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseTbSummary: Infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis that requires specialized care.
dashboardPatientAiDiseaseTbSigns: Typical signs include persistent cough, night sweats, and unexplained weight loss.

legalTermsBody:
TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Asclepio Digital Medical Platform
Version 1.0 - Date: April 20, 2026

1. ACCEPTANCE
By accessing the Asclepio platform, the user accepts these Terms, which govern the use of triage microservices, AI, Docker, and Terraform, in compliance with Colombian laws such as Law 1581 of 2012 and Law 23 of 1981.

2. SERVICE DESCRIPTION
- Digital medical services: registration, authentication, appointments, triage, MongoDB storage, and communication.

3. USER OBLIGATIONS
- Provide truthful information.
- Use the platform ethically and lawfully.
- Maintain confidentiality.

4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
- No liability for errors in automated diagnoses, interruptions, or indirect damages.
- Use is at the user's own risk.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
- The platform and its content are property of Asclepio.

6. TERMINATION
- Asclepio may terminate access for violations, with notice.

7. APPLICABLE LAW
- Colombian legislation.

8. CHANGES
- Terms are updated with prior notice.

9. CONTACT
legal@asclepio.com
legalUsageBody:
USAGE POLICIES
Asclepio Digital Medical Platform
Version 1.0 - Date: April 20, 2026

1. INTRODUCTION
These Usage Policies regulate access to and use of the Asclepio digital medical platform, including triage microservices, data validation, local AI integration (Whisper), and deployment with Docker and Terraform. Users include patients, doctors, insurers, and administrative staff. These policies comply with Law 23 of 1981 (medical ethics) and promote responsible use of the platform.

2. AUTHORIZED USERS
- Patients: Limited access to their profile, triage, appointments, and medical history.
- Doctors: Management of patients, appointments, availability, and medical records.
- Insurers: Data consultation for coverage validation.
- Administrative Staff: User administration, inventories, and reports.

3. RULES OF USE
3.1 Permitted Use
- Secure registration and authentication.
- Requesting and managing medical appointments.
- Automated triage with AI.
- Secure chat/video communication.
- Access to medical records with a minimum 20-year retention period.

3.2 Prohibited Use
- Sharing credentials or accessing other users' data.
- Attempting to compromise system security (e.g., hacking, code injection).
- Using the platform for non-medical or illegal purposes.
- Altering medical data without authorization.

4. USER RESPONSIBILITIES
- Provide truthful and up-to-date information.
- Immediately report any suspected security breach.
- Comply with medical ethics and confidentiality rules.

5. SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
- The platform may suspend or terminate access for policy violations, with prior notice.

6. DISCLAIMERS
- The platform does not guarantee uninterrupted availability or absolute accuracy of automated diagnoses.
- Users assume the risks of service interruptions or AI errors.

7. UPDATES
These policies are updated according to regulatory or technological changes.

8. CONTACT
For inquiries: soporte@asclepio.com
legalSecurityBody:
SECURITY POLICY
Asclepio Digital Medical Platform
Version 1.0 - Date: April 20, 2026

1. INTRODUCTION
This Security Policy establishes the measures and procedures implemented to protect the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of the information handled on the Asclepio digital medical platform. The platform uses triage microservices, strict data validation, local AI integration (Whisper), and reproducible deployment with Docker and Terraform. This policy complies with applicable Colombian regulations, including Law 23 of 1981 (medical ethics) and Resolution 1995 of 1999 (medical record management).

2. OBJECTIVES
- Ensure the protection of sensitive health data, personal information, and access credentials.
- Prevent unauthorized access, alteration, or data loss.
- Ensure service continuity in flows such as user registration, authentication, appointment requests, automated triage, MongoDB storage, and chat/video communication.
- Comply with digital security standards, including encryption, access control, and audits.

3. TECHNICAL SECURITY MEASURES
3.1 Data Encryption
- Sensitive data is encrypted in transit and at rest using AES-256 standards.
- Communications between microservices and users are protected using TLS 1.3.
- Access credentials are stored as hashes with secure algorithms (e.g., bcrypt).

3.2 Access Control
- Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented for all users (patients, doctors, insurers, and administrative staff).
- Role-based access controls under the least-privilege principle (RBAC).
- User sessions expire automatically after inactivity and JWT tokens have limited lifetimes.

3.3 Audits and Monitoring
- All system activity is logged in auditable logs, with 20-year retention for medical records as required by Resolution 1995 of 1999.
- Continuous monitoring with intrusion detection tools and automatic alerts.
- Periodic third-party audits to verify compliance.

3.4 Infrastructure Security
- Deployment in Docker containers with verified images scanned for vulnerabilities.
- Use of Terraform for reproducible and secure cloud configurations.
- Encrypted and geographically distributed backups, with regular restore tests.

4. OPERATIONAL SECURITY MEASURES
4.1 Training
- All employees and technology partners receive annual training in information security and medical ethics.

4.2 Incident Management
- Security incident response procedures include immediate notification to affected users and competent authorities in case of data breaches.

4.3 Data Transfers
- Any transfer of data to third parties (insurers or technology partners) requires explicit consent and secure protocols.

5. DISCLAIMERS
- The platform assumes no responsibility for errors in AI-generated automated diagnoses, service interruptions due to external failures, or misuse by users.
- Users are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of their credentials.

6. UPDATES
This policy is reviewed annually or whenever significant regulatory or technological changes occur.

7. CONTACT
For security inquiries: seguridad@asclepio.com
legalPrivacyBody:
PRIVACY POLICY AND PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING
Asclepio Digital Medical Platform
Version 1.0 - Date: April 20, 2026

1. INTRODUCTION
This Privacy Policy regulates the processing of personal data on the Asclepio digital medical platform, in compliance with Law 1581 of 2012 (personal data protection in Colombia). The platform handles personal information, sensitive health data, medical records, and credentials in flows such as registration, authentication, triage, and communication.

2. COLLECTED DATA
- Personal information: name, ID number, contact details.
- Sensitive data: medical records, triage results, vital signs.
- Technical data: access logs, IP, devices.

3. PURPOSES OF PROCESSING
- Medical service delivery: registration, appointments, automated triage.
- Communication: secure chat/video communication.
- Regulatory compliance: 20-year retention for medical records (Resolution 1995 of 1999).
- Third-party integration: insurers and technology partners, with consent.

4. LEGAL BASIS
- Informed consent from the data subject.
- Compliance with legal obligations (Law 23 of 1981, medical ethics).

5. DATA SUBJECT RIGHTS
- Access, rectification, deletion, objection, and portability of data.
- Withdrawal of consent at any time.

6. DATA TRANSFER
- Data is transferred to third parties only with authorization and under secure protocols (encryption, confidentiality agreements).

7. SECURITY MEASURES
- AES-256 encryption, RBAC access controls, audits, and monitoring.

8. RETENTION
- Personal data: until consent is withdrawn.
- Medical records: minimum of 20 years.

9. DISCLAIMERS
- The platform is not responsible for losses resulting from unauthorized access caused by user negligence.

10. UPDATES
This policy is updated annually.

11. CONTACT
Data protection officer: privacidad@asclepio.com
